summary: Good‑quality audience recording of a strong ’83 show – fiery Jack Straw, soaring Cassidy and a standout Space→Truckin’ transition.
review: The tape captures an energetic middle night of the 1983 run with the band in solid pocket; highlights include Jack Straw, Loser, Cassidy, China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider and a thrilling Bertha plus Space‑>Truckin’. Sound is decent for an audience source – clear enough to enjoy, though the treble is bright, mids are thin and there are occasional clipping/pitch quirks.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers rate the show 4‑star level and praise musical peaks while noting only moderate recording flaws; the consistent positivity yields a solid above‑average rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Treble a bit harsh, mids thin and some clipping/pitch errors on certain tape sections; overall clear enough to follow the performance with decent stereo image for an audience source.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set rolls smoothly with fire‑y Jack Straw and a soaring Cassidy; second set is mixed – high points like Bertha and the Space→Truckin’ jam, but some sections described as average ’83 material.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s tone is bright (treble a touch harsh) yet his playing is energetic – “Jer leaves it all onstage” especially on Loser and during Space.
band_member_comments.Phil: Bass presence finally comes through (“Phill feeling”) and Phil is described as having fun, adding solid groove to the set.
